title: hibbertia echiifolia subsp. cernua
intro: Hibbertia echiifolia subsp. cernua, commonly known as the drooping Guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia echiifolia subsp. cernua include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
